summary: Validated 3 adjustments — (1) engine_cc & transmission on Car/Motorcycle items, (2) Article 4 2-month interest cap on overdue contracts, (3) Contracts month filter dropdown. Backend: 11/11 pytest cases pass. Frontend: all 3 flows verified in the browser — Car & Motorcycle dialogs render the two new fields (Manual/Automatic options confirmed), Month filter updates the URL to ?month=YYYY-MM (261 rows for 2026-07, 0 mismatches), filter pill shows, Clear filter × removes BOTH month and status params. Deep-link ?status=auction_ready still works. Finance Invoice PDF preview modal opens with iframe. No regressions detected.

issues: Recharts prints 'width(-1) height(-1)' warnings on initial render for the ResponsiveContainer charts — cosmetic, not blocking.

design_issues.screen: Multiple Dialogs (Car dialog, Motorcycle dialog, PDF preview modal)
issues: Missing aria-describedby / DialogDescription warnings from Radix — accessibility polish only. Contract dialog and Reactivate dialog already have sr-only DialogDescription; Items dialogs do not.

action_items: OPTIONAL: add DialogDescription (sr-only) to the Items New/Edit dialog and to the PDF preview dialog to silence the Radix a11y warning.
critical_code_review_comments: services.py cap: MAX_BILLING_MONTHS=2 applied AFTER computing months_elapsed_uncapped and BEFORE the anchor loop — correct: interest_owed accumulates over at most 2 iterations, per_month_billed has exactly 2 entries, and next_interest_date uses the capped months_elapsed which means it will keep advancing to today for very-overdue contracts (acceptable per Article 4 since no further interest accrues anyway).
The overdue contract test proves the cap: days_overdue=~940 with contract_date=2024-01-01 and due_date=2024-01-31 → months_elapsed=2, interest_charged=$200 (=2×1000×10%), per_month_billed=[100,100].
pdf_utils.py Article 2: 'interese fulan 2 maximu' present at line 279, no 'fulan tolu' remaining anywhere in the file (verified via grep). PDF for existing overdue contracts renders 200 OK.

server.py CarIn / MotorcycleIn correctly declare engine_cc: Optional[int] and transmission: str = '' — Pydantic accepts payloads without them (backward compat verified). Values persist through POST → GET → PUT → GET.

Contracts.js month filter: monthOptions is derived from the loaded rows (sorted descending), which is correct. Filter is a pure client-side predicate (r.contract_date.startsWith(ym)) — no backend query, so no perf/correctness risk. Clear button rebuilds URLSearchParams from scratch (drops both status and month) — matches spec.

success_rate.frontend: 100% for tested flows (Items Car/Motorcycle dialogs, Contracts month filter with URL sync + pill + clear, deep-link status filter, Finance invoice PDF preview modal)

seed_data_creation: Backend test creates 1 TEST_ client + 1 TEST_ car + 1 TEST_ contract (contract_date=2024-01-01, due_date=2024-01-31) to prove the 2-month cap on a real overdue record, then deletes all three at class teardown. Also creates and deletes 3 temporary items (2 cars + 1 motorcycle) for field-persistence assertions.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Regression file at /app/backend/tests/test_iter33_month_cap_and_vehicle_fields.py has 4 classes: TestVehicleFields (3 tests — car+motorcycle engine_cc/transmission POST/PUT/GET + legacy backward-compat), TestArticle4Cap (3 tests — months_elapsed==2, interest_charged==2×loan×rate%, per_month_billed length==2), TestContractPdfArticle2Wording (1 test — no 'fulan tolu' in PDF bytes), TestRegression (4 tests — /reports/v2/financial, /finance/summary, /dashboard/summary, and a systemwide check that no live contract has months_elapsed>2). Frontend testids used: item-car-engine_cc, item-car-transmission, item-motorcycle-engine_cc, item-motorcycle-transmission, contracts-month-filter, contracts-month-option-{YYYY-MM}, contracts-month-option-all, contracts-filter-pill, contracts-filter-clear. Note about the pre-existing test failure mentioned in the request (backend_test.py::TestAuctionFlow::test_overdue_to_auction_sold): NOT re-run in this iteration since it's called out as expected-pre-existing. All new tests pass.
